findings.message: session.commit() is called inside the loop, destroying atomicity across the batch. If any subsequent insert fails, all previously committed items remain in the database — a partial write that leaves the store in an inconsistent state with no way to roll back the already-committed rows.
findings.suggestion: Move all session.add() calls before a single session.commit() after the loop: add all items, then commit once, then refresh all. Alternatively, use session.add_all(items) followed by a single commit to ensure the entire batch is atomic.

findings.message: The function has no return statement. It always returns None instead of the list[Item] declared in the signature, silently dropping all results for every caller.
findings.suggestion: Add 'return created' after the loop body.
